sentences.sentence: In the Scottish Highlands there, is believed to be a monster Jiving in the waters of Loch Ness.
sentences.translation: 人们相信，在苏格兰高地的尼斯湖水域中生活着一只怪兽。

structure.text: In the Scottish Highlands
there, is believed to be a monster
Jiving in the waters of Loch Ness

difficult_points: there is believed to be 是被动存在句，意为“据说有”。
原文 there 后多出逗号，Jiving 很可能是 living 的扫描错误，均须原样保留。

sentences.sentence: The waters of Loch Ness are one mile wide and 24 miles long, the largest body of freshwater in the United Kingdom.
sentences.translation: 尼斯湖宽1英里、长24英里，是英国最大的淡水水体。

structure.text: The waters of Loch Ness
are one mile wide and 24 miles long
the largest body of freshwater in the United Kingdom

difficult_points: one mile wide 与 24 miles long 是并列表语。
逗号后的名词短语是补充同位说明，语义上指 Loch Ness。

expressions.text: one mile wide and 24 miles long

sentences.sentence: To many sceptics, this monster is only a modem day myth, but to others who claim they have seen it, the monster exists and still lurks in the waters of Loch Ness today.
sentences.translation: 对许多怀疑者而言，这只怪兽只是一个现代神话；但对那些声称亲眼见过它的人来说，怪兽确实存在，如今仍潜伏在尼斯湖水中。

structure.text: To many sceptics
this monster is only a modem day myth
but to others
who claim they have seen it
the monster exists and still lurks in the waters of Loch Ness today

difficult_points: but 对比怀疑者和目击者两种观点；who 从句修饰 others。
claim 后省略 that；原文 modem day 很可能是 modern-day 的误写。

sentences.sentence: Throughout Scotland, research has been done at several lakes but Loch Ness Is the icon for monsters – Nessie, as the monster is affectionately named, being the most popular of them all.
sentences.translation: 苏格兰各地多个湖泊都开展过相关研究，但尼斯湖已成为怪兽传说的标志——这只怪兽被亲切地称为“尼斯”，是所有湖怪中最著名的。

structure.text: Throughout Scotland
research has been done at several lakes
but Loch Ness Is the icon for monsters
Nessie
as the monster is affectionately named
being the most popular of them all

difficult_points: 破折号后的 Nessie ... being ... 是独立主格，补充说明尼斯湖怪最受欢迎。
as 从句意为“正如这只怪兽被亲切称呼的那样”。原文 Is 异常大写，需保留。

expressions.text: the most popular of them all

sentences.sentence: Both professionals and amateurs flock to this lake with their cameras in the hope of capturing a brief glimpse of Nessie and possibly solving the mystery of the Loch Ness monster.
sentences.translation: 专业人士和业余爱好者都带着相机蜂拥至此，希望短暂拍到尼斯湖怪，并可能解开这一谜团。

structure.text: Both professionals and amateurs
flock to this lake
with their cameras
in the hope of capturing a brief glimpse of Nessie and possibly solving the mystery of the Loch Ness monster

difficult_points: Both ... and ... 连接两类主语。
in the hope of 后接两个并列动名词 capturing 和 solving。

sentences.sentence: The very first sighting of Nessie was as far back as 565 AD.
sentences.translation: 最早的尼斯湖怪目击记录可追溯至公元565年。

structure.text: The very first sighting of Nessie
was as far back as 565 AD

difficult_points: very 强调 first，表示最早的一次。
as far back as 强调时间极为久远。

sentences.sentence: It is believed that she ate a local farmer and then dived back into the waters, with no accounts of being seen again for over 100 years.
sentences.translation: 据说它吃掉了一名当地农民，随后潜回水中，此后一百多年再无目击记录。

structure.text: It is believed
that she ate a local farmer and then dived back into the waters
with no accounts of being seen again for over 100 years

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，that 从句是真正主语。
with no accounts ... 表示伴随结果，being seen 是被动动名词。

sentences.sentence: However, since the turn of the 20th century, several other people have claimed to see her.
sentences.translation: 然而，自20世纪之交以来，又有数人声称见过它。

structure.text: since the turn of the 20th century
several other people
have claimed to see her

difficult_points: since 与现在完成时 have claimed 搭配，表示从过去延续至今。
the turn of the 20th century 指19世纪末20世纪初。

expressions.text: since the turn of the century

sentences.sentence: Some people believe that old Scottish myths about water creatures such as Kelpies and water horses have contributed to the idea of tills wondrous monster lurking beneath.
sentences.translation: 一些人认为，关于凯尔派和水马等水中生物的古老苏格兰神话，促成了人们对潜伏水下的这只神奇怪兽的想象。

structure.text: Some people believe
that old Scottish myths about water creatures such as Kelpies and water horses have contributed to the idea
of tills wondrous monster lurking beneath
lurking beneath

difficult_points: that 从句作 believe 的宾语；such as 后列举神话水怪。
lurking beneath 修饰 monster。原文 tills 很可能是 this 的误写，必须保留。

sentences.sentence: 2007 brought about the most recent sighting of Nessie.
sentences.translation: 2007年出现了最近一次尼斯湖怪目击事件。

structure.text: brought about the most recent sighting of Nessie

difficult_points: 年份 2007 被拟人化作主语。
bring about 在此表示带来或发生，而不是人为促成。

sentences.sentence: A tourist named Gordon Holmes from Yorkshire visited the Loch Ness waters and claims lie not only saw her, but has captured her on video.
sentences.translation: 一名来自约克郡、名叫戈登·霍姆斯的游客来到尼斯湖，并声称自己不仅看见了它，还拍下了视频。

structure.text: A tourist
named Gordon Holmes from Yorkshire
visited the Loch Ness waters
and claims lie not only saw her, but has captured her on video

difficult_points: named ... 修饰 A tourist；visited 与 claims 是并列谓语，但时态不一致。
原文 lie 很可能是 he，且 not only ... but ... 内 saw/has captured 时态不一致，均须保留。

sentences.sentence: He claimed she was jet black, about 15 metres long and travelling in a very straight line at about 6 miles per hour.
sentences.translation: 他声称，它通体漆黑，约15米长，并以每小时约6英里的速度沿直线移动。

structure.text: He claimed
she was jet black, about 15 metres long and travelling in a very straight line
at about 6 miles per hour

difficult_points: claimed 后省略 that；jet black、15 metres long 和 travelling ... 是并列表语。
at ... miles per hour 表示移动速度。

sentences.sentence: Despite this supposed evidence, controversy still reigns and opinion clearly divided about its existence.
sentences.translation: 尽管有这项所谓证据，争议仍占主导，关于它是否存在的意见显然分裂。

structure.text: Despite this supposed evidence
controversy still reigns
and opinion clearly divided about its existence

difficult_points: Despite 后接名词短语表达让步。
原文 opinion clearly divided 缺少 is，语法不完整但必须保留；its 指怪兽。

sentences.sentence: The video footage has been discredited amid accusations of tampering, whilst others claim mat the image caught by Holmes could be nothing more than a tree trunk or even one of the otters that inhabit the loch.
sentences.translation: 在篡改指控之下，这段视频已失去可信度；另一些人则声称，霍姆斯拍到的影像可能只是一根树干，甚至只是栖息在湖中的一只水獭。

structure.text: The video footage has been discredited
amid accusations of tampering
whilst others claim mat the image caught by Holmes could be nothing more than a tree trunk or even one of the otters
caught by Holmes
that inhabit the loch

difficult_points: whilst 引出其他人的不同解释；caught by Holmes 修饰 image。
that 从句修饰 otters。原文 mat 很可能是 that 的误写，必须保留。

sentences.sentence: Some scientists believe that the Loch Ness monster could a mirage or a psychological phenomenon in as much as sometimes we see what we want to see.
sentences.translation: 一些科学家认为，尼斯湖怪可能是一种海市蜃楼或心理现象，因为有时我们看到的只是自己想看到的东西。

structure.text: Some scientists believe
that the Loch Ness monster could a mirage or a psychological phenomenon
in as much as sometimes we see what we want to see
what we want to see

difficult_points: that 从句作 believe 的宾语；in as much as 在此引出原因。
what 从句作 see 的宾语。原文 could 后缺少 be，须原样保留。

sentences.sentence: Of course those who have seen her beg to differ, but many experiments have been conducted to arrive at theories to explain what it is that people could be witnessing.
sentences.translation: 当然，声称见过它的人并不同意；但研究者进行了许多实验，试图形成理论来解释人们可能看到的究竟是什么。

structure.text: those
who have seen her
beg to differ
but many experiments have been conducted
to arrive at theories to explain what it is that people could be witnessing

difficult_points: who 从句修饰 those；beg to differ 是礼貌表达不同意。
两个不定式逐层表示实验目的；what it is that ... 是强调式嵌入问句。

sentences.sentence: It has been suggested that Nessie could be related to a prehistoric animal known as a Plesiosaur, an animal that measured up to ten metres in length and is otherwise thought to be extinct, although this theory is unsupported by any data, One scientist in particular has been researching the lake itself to find out more about its history, It seems that for such a large animal to Jive in this lake it would require a vast food source, but for such an amount of fish to survive there would need to be plenty of microscopic animals called zooplankton.
sentences.translation: 有人提出，尼斯湖怪可能与一种叫作蛇颈龙的史前动物有关；这种动物体长可达10米，通常被认为已经灭绝，尽管这一理论没有任何数据支持。一位科学家一直在专门研究湖泊本身以了解其历史。如此大的动物若要生活在湖中，需要庞大的食物来源；而要让这么多鱼存活，又必须有大量称为浮游动物的微小生物。

structure.text: It has been suggested
that Nessie could be related to a prehistoric animal known as a Plesiosaur
an animal that measured up to ten metres in length and is otherwise thought to be extinct
although this theory is unsupported by any data
One scientist in particular has been researching the lake itself
to find out more about its history
It seems that for such a large animal to Jive in this lake it would require a vast food source
but for such an amount of fish to survive there would need to be plenty of microscopic animals called zooplankton

difficult_points: 源文用逗号错误串联三个本应独立的句子，必须作为一个 Block 保留。
known as ... 修饰 animal；后面的 an animal 是 Plesiosaur 的同位语。
for such a large animal to Jive ... 是不定式复合结构；Jive 很可能是 live 的误写。
called zooplankton 修饰 microscopic animals；there would need to be 是存在句。

sentences.sentence: The only way to find out how much of this there is in the water is to measure the amount of algae.
sentences.translation: 要查明水中有多少这类生物，唯一的方法是测量藻类的数量。

structure.text: The only way to find out how much of this there is in the water
how much of this there is in the water
is to measure the amount of algae

difficult_points: 主干是 The only way ... is to measure ...，两个不定式分别修饰 way 和作表语。
how much 从句作 find out 的宾语，this 回指 zooplankton。

expressions.text: the only way to find out

sentences.sentence: Algae needs light to survive so by measuring just how deep the daylight can penetrate the lake scientists can then start to work out what kind of population can be sustained.
sentences.translation: 藻类需要光才能存活，因此，通过测量日光能穿透湖水多深，科学家便可以开始推算湖中能够维持怎样规模的生物种群。

structure.text: Algae needs light to survive
so by measuring just how deep the daylight can penetrate the lake
scientists can then start to work out
what kind of population can be sustained

difficult_points: so 引出研究推理，by + 动名词说明推算方式。
how deep 从句作 measuring 的宾语，what kind 从句作 work out 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: Despite results that suggest that the fish population was too small, the conclusions drawn were inconclusive.
sentences.translation: 尽管结果显示鱼类种群太小，但所得结论仍不确定。

structure.text: Despite results
that suggest that the fish population was too small
the conclusions
drawn
were inconclusive

difficult_points: 第一个 that 修饰 results，第二个 that 从句作 suggest 的宾语。
drawn 修饰 conclusions；inconclusive 表示尚不能形成确定结论。

sentences.sentence: In the 1900s, a ten year observational study was carried out, recording an average of 20 sightings of Nessie per year, The phenomenon exploded in second half of the century, when photos were publicly released of a 'flipper', Submarines were sent into die Loch Ness to try to discover more about this creature (and now are actually run as tourist attractions).
sentences.translation: 20世纪开展了一项为期十年的观察研究，平均每年记录20次尼斯湖怪目击。到该世纪后半叶，随着一张所谓“鳍状肢”的照片公开，相关现象迅速升温；潜水艇也被派入尼斯湖，试图进一步探查这种生物（如今潜水艇项目实际上被作为旅游景点运营）。

structure.text: a ten year observational study was carried out
recording an average of 20 sightings of Nessie per year
The phenomenon exploded in second half of the century
when photos were publicly released of a 'flipper'
Submarines were sent into die Loch Ness
to try to discover more about this creature
and now are actually run as tourist attractions

difficult_points: 源文用逗号串联三个本应独立的句子，因此需整体保留为一个 Block。
recording ... 表示研究所记录的结果；when 从句说明现象升温的时间背景。
原文 ten year 缺连字符、in second half 缺 the、die Loch Ness 很可能是 the Loch Ness，均须保留。

expressions.text: an average of 20 sightings per year

sentences.sentence: Another theory behind why Nessie rose to the top of the waters where she was more likely to be seen, was that disruption from nearby road works in the 1930s forced her to move to higher levels due to the amount of vibration fell in the water.
sentences.translation: 关于尼斯湖怪为何会上浮到更容易被看见的水面，还有一种理论：20世纪30年代附近道路施工造成的干扰和水中振动，迫使它游到较高水层。

structure.text: Another theory behind why Nessie rose to the top of the waters
why Nessie rose to the top of the waters
where she was more likely to be seen
was
that disruption from nearby road works in the 1930s forced her to move to higher levels
due to the amount of vibration fell in the water

difficult_points: why 从句作 behind 的宾语，where 从句修饰 waters。
that 从句作表语；force + 宾语 + to do 表示迫使移动。
原文主语 theory 后多余逗号，vibration fell in the water 结构也不完整，须原样保留。

expressions.text: be more likely to be seen

sentences.sentence: Another argument centres on the geographical placement of the Loch, which sits on the Great Glen fault line formed over 400 million years ago.
sentences.translation: 另一种观点聚焦于尼斯湖的地理位置：它坐落在形成于4亿多年前的大峡谷断层线上。

structure.text: Another argument
centres on the geographical placement of the Loch
which sits on the Great Glen fault line
formed over 400 million years ago

difficult_points: which 引导非限制性定语从句，先行词是 the Loch。
formed ... 后置修饰 fault line。

sentences.sentence: Some scientists have claimed that resulting seismic activity in the lake could cause disturbances on its surface and people could be mistaking this for Nessie.
sentences.translation: 一些科学家声称，由此产生的湖中地震活动可能造成水面扰动，人们可能把这种现象误认为尼斯湖怪。

structure.text: Some scientists have claimed
that resulting seismic activity in the lake could cause disturbances on its surface
and people could be mistaking this for Nessie

difficult_points: that 从句作 claimed 的宾语，内部由 and 连接两项因果链条。
mistake A for B 表示把 A 误认为 B；this 指水面扰动。

sentences.sentence: Up until today, there is no convincing proof to suggest that the monster is real, which, given the preponderance of digital cameras, webcams trained on the loch and other technological advances in recording equipment, suggests that the myth of Nessie may be just that – a myth.
sentences.translation: 直到今天，仍没有令人信服的证据表明怪兽真实存在；考虑到数码相机的大量普及、对准湖面的网络摄像头以及其他记录设备的技术进步，这反而表明尼斯湖怪传说或许真的只是传说。

structure.text: Up until today
there is no convincing proof
to suggest that the monster is real
which
given the preponderance of digital cameras, webcams trained on the loch and other technological advances in recording equipment
suggests that the myth of Nessie may be just that – a myth

difficult_points: to suggest ... 修饰 proof；第一个 that 从句作 suggest 的宾语。
which 回指没有确证这一整件事，given ... 表示“考虑到”。
just that – a myth 中 that 指前面的 myth，并由破折号再次强调。

expressions.meaning_zh: 没有令人信服的证据表明

sentences.sentence: Nonetheless, accommodation all over the Scottish Highlands offers tours of the lake itself so tourists can try to catch their own glimpse of Nessie, In 2007, it was estimated that related tourism brought in an estimated £6 million to the region, thanks to the attention of the film industry.
sentences.translation: 尽管如此，苏格兰高地各处的住宿机构仍提供尼斯湖游览项目，让游客有机会亲自一睹尼斯湖怪。2007年，据估计相关旅游业为当地带来了约600万英镑收入，这得益于电影业的关注。

structure.text: accommodation all over the Scottish Highlands
offers tours of the lake itself
so tourists can try to catch their own glimpse of Nessie
In 2007, it was estimated
that related tourism brought in an estimated £6 million to the region
thanks to the attention of the film industry

difficult_points: so 从句说明湖区游览项目的目的或结果。
原文在 Nessie 后用逗号错误连接新句，因此两部分必须作为同一 Block 保留。
第二部分 it 是形式主语，that 从句是真正主语；bring in 表示带来收入。
